---
title: "Show Hidden Files on a Mac"
date: 2010-11-16
author: Jonathan Whitmore
draft: true
description: "Two Terminal commands to toggle the display of hidden dot-files in macOS Finder, and why killall Finder is needed to apply the change immediately."
categories: [macos, tools]
---

How to show hidden files on a Mac:

Terminal:

```
defaults write com.apple.finder AppleShowAllFiles TRUE
killall Finder
```

The killall command kills and refreshes all open Finder windows.

If you would like to turn it back off:

```
defaults write com.apple.finder AppleShowAllFiles FALSE
killall Finder
```
